message:
test(harness): conformance ext4 i_flags / uid / gid / mode-perms vs debugfs

Four POSIX-visible inode fields had no end-to-end conformance pin:

  * `i_flags` (the EXT4_*_FL bits chattr exposes — IMMUTABLE,
    APPEND_ONLY, NOATIME, EXTENTS, INDEX, HUGE_FILE, …)
  * `i_uid` / `i_gid` across the lo/hi-half split (i_uid_hi at
    inode byte 0x74, i_gid_hi at 0x72) — values > u16::MAX exercise
    the high-half reassembly the lo-only path could silently drop
  * the permission half of `i_mode` (the bottom 12 bits including
    setuid / setgid / sticky) — kernel_reference's inode_metadata
    test verifies only the type half via `ext4_file_type_str`

A regression in any of these would silently corrupt every `stat(2)`
call: mis-reported chattr protections, wrong file ownership, or
lost setuid/setgid/sticky bits on disk.

New harness ext4_inode_flags_uidgid_kernel_reference stages eight
cases that explicitly exercise each surface:

  * defaults (only EXTENTS_FL)
  * append-only + immutable (chattr +ai)
  * HUGE_FILE | EXTENTS — high-byte + low-byte flag bits together
  * 16-bit uid/gid (lo-only path)
  * uid/gid that require i_uid_hi / i_gid_hi
  * u32::MAX uid/gid — boundary case (debugfs renders these as
    signed `Group: -1`, so the parser accepts both unsigned and
    signed-cast forms via from_le_bytes/to_le_bytes round-trip)
  * mode 0o755 — plain rwxr-xr-x
  * setuid + setgid + sticky on a regular file (0o107755)

Cross-corpus invariants enforce that the high-half uid path, the
upper-byte flag path, and the setuid/setgid/sticky permission path
are each exercised by at least one case, so a future cleanup that
drops one shape silently weakens the test.

debugfs's `set_inode_field … mode` parses C-style octal (leading
`0`) and rejects Rust's `{:#o}` (`0o…`) prefix; the harness format
sets the mode via `0{mode:o}` to avoid silent no-op mutations.

Self-skips when ext4 userland is unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
